mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower expected earnings growth over short horizons.
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of observed instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 310, or rank IC against CPIAUCSL is not reliably negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals labor market weakness and impending recession, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings expectations and negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the 60-day forward cumulative return is positive in more than 60% of cases, implying the threshold fails to identify impending downturns.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous presence of high inflation and a very tight labor market intensifies hawkish central bank expectations, raising real rates and risk premiums, which depresses equity prices in the near term.
failure_condition: The joint trigger produces 20-day positive returns more than half the time, or the signal is indistinguishable from a single-variable model (interaction term not significant in a linear regression).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, which in a late-cycle environment foreshadows a broader economic contraction and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: After a decline trigger, the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and income, supporting consumption and corporate earnings, which leads to positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS level and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or the correlation is not reliably positive in expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, pushing up corporate revenues and near-term profit expectations, which quickly lifts equity prices.
failure_condition: In triggered weeks, the 5-day forward return is negative more than half the time, or the average excess return over non-triggered weeks is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When interest rates are restrictive and industrial production is already contracting, the economy faces a monetary overhang that suppresses investment and output, producing sustained negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint trigger yields 60-day positive returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a regression adds no predictive power beyond the individual series.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the economy is expanding (as proxied by positive GDP growth), increases in industrial production reinforce the growth narrative and are rewarded with short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: In the expansion regime, the 5-day forward return after INDPRO increases is not significantly positive, or the strategy fails to outperform a buy-and-hold over a full cycle.
